name: Counterparty Default Features
summary: Build a counterparty-default feature set: join trades to the counterparty master, derive days_since_last_trade as a GREL formula, fill missing credit ratings with NR, qcut-bin current_exposure into Q1..Q5, one-hot encode credit_rating, drop nulls.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Trade ledger and counterparty master.
# counterparty_master.csv: counterparty_id, name, country, credit_rating,
#   exposure_limit, current_exposure, master_agreement (ISDA / NAESB / EFET / GTMA),
#   last_review_date, watch_list_flag
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
counterparties = pd.read_csv("counterparty_master.csv")
features = trades.merge(counterparties, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Days since the last trade against today's reference date.
features = features.assign(
    days_since_last_trade=lambda x: x["trade_date"] - "2026-04-26",
)

# 'NR' is the standard not-rated code.
features["credit_rating"] = features["credit_rating"].fillna("NR")

# Quintile bucket on current exposure.
features["exposure_bucket"] = pd.qcut(
    features["current_exposure"],
    q=5,
    labels=["Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q5"],
)

# One-hot encode the credit rating.
features = pd.get_dummies(features, columns=["credit_rating"])

# Drop rows with no recent activity signal.
features = features.dropna(subset=["days_since_last_trade"])
features.to_parquet("counterparty_features.parquet")

name: Counterparty Exposure Roll-Up
summary: Join trades to the counterparty master, roll up notional and trade-count by (counterparty_id, master_agreement, credit_rating), then rank the 25 largest counterparty exposures for the credit committee.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Trades and the counterparty master share counterparty_id.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
counterparties = pd.read_csv("counterparty_master.csv")

# Enrich each trade with its counterparty record.
joined = trades.merge(counterparties, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Roll up exposure by (counterparty, master agreement, rating bucket).
rollup = joined.groupby([
    "counterparty_id",
    "master_agreement",
    "credit_rating",
]).agg({
    "notional": "sum",
    "trade_id": "count",
}).reset_index()

# Top 25 counterparties by aggregate notional.
ranked = rollup.nlargest(25, "notional")
ranked.to_csv("counterparty_exposure_rollup.csv", index=False)

name: Counterparty Limit Monitoring
summary: Join trades to the counterparty exposure-limit register, derive utilisation_pct via GREL, gate to >80% utilisation and rank worst-first — the credit-risk alert queue.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Live trade tape + the counterparty exposure-limit register.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
limits = pd.read_csv("counterparty_limits.csv")

# Enrich trades with each counterparty's limit.
joined = trades.merge(limits, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Utilisation = current_exposure / exposure_limit, expressed as a percent.
joined = joined.assign(
    utilization_pct=lambda x: (x["current_exposure"] / x["exposure_limit"]) * 100,
)

# Threshold: anything above 80% utilisation hits the credit-risk alert queue.
alerts = joined[joined["utilization_pct"] > 80]

# Worst breaches first.
ranked = alerts.sort_values("utilization_pct", ascending=False)
ranked.to_csv("limit_alerts.csv", index=False)

name: Master-Agreement Coverage Audit
summary: Join trades to the counterparty master, then flag rows where the master agreement is missing OR the last review date is older than 18 months — the credit-ops coverage audit.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Audit: which trades are covered by an in-force master agreement?
# counterparty_master.csv: counterparty_id, master_agreement (ISDA/NAESB/EFET),
#   last_review_date, watch_list_flag, exposure_limit.
trades = pd.read_csv("trades.csv")
counterparty_master = pd.read_csv("counterparty_master.csv")

# Pull each trade's master-agreement record.
joined = trades.merge(counterparty_master, on="counterparty_id", how="left")

# Flag rows where the master is missing OR the last review is stale (>540 days).
flagged = joined[
    (joined["master_agreement"].isna())
    | (joined["last_review_date"] < "2024-10-26")
]

flagged.to_csv("master_agreement_gaps.csv", index=False)

name: VaR Input Preparation
summary: Roll positions up by (book, commodity, tenor), pivot tenor wide so each (book, commodity) is one row of the VaR-engine input grid — the canonical overnight VaR feed shape.
pythonSource:
import pandas as pd

# Position tape — input to the overnight VaR engine.
# positions.csv: position_id, book, commodity, tenor, notional,
#   delta, gamma, vega, region.
positions = pd.read_csv("positions.csv")

# Sum notional per (book, commodity, tenor) — the natural VaR risk-bucket key.
by_bucket = positions.groupby(["book", "commodity", "tenor"]).agg({
    "notional": "sum",
}).reset_index()

# Pivot tenor to columns so the VaR engine ingests one row per (book, commodity).
var_input = by_bucket.pivot(
    index=["book", "commodity"],
    columns="tenor",
    values="notional",
)

var_input.to_csv("var_input.csv")
